text:
I once lost myself exploring an old bookstore. The scent of aged books filled my lungs. Some books smelled musty – old stories untold. Others were fresh from new prints. I picked up a dusty tome about sailing voyages. The sailor spoke bravely to the ocean winds.

You may think you know what you want. That’s why you buy that fancy dress you never wear. It makes you feel… different. We’re the sailors. We go where the wind takes us.

I wandered amongst the shadows. A small creature scuttled from my boot heel. It was a mouse. I hadn’t meant to hurt it.

Oh dear. I feel terrible.

We returned to my room. I lowered the book. The mouse glared back and hopped away. I’ll apologise later.

For now, I’ll read their story. The voyager lived forever. Until his ship struck an iceberg…<|endoftext|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|><|pad|>
